description: DALP Platform Walkthrough deck covering all major platform features from login through governance and audit.

slides.title: DALP Platform Walkthrough
slides.subtitle: Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform, Feature Overview

slides.title: Platform Entry
slides.subtitle: Secure access and real-time command centre

slides.title: Secure Authentication
slides.body: DALP supports passwordless passkey login alongside email/password. Access is role-gated from the first click, ensuring only authorised participants reach the platform.

slides.title: Real-Time Operations Dashboard
slides.body: The dashboard gives operators a live command centre: portfolio valuation, AUM tracking, pending action alerts, and a blockchain activity feed. The sidebar exposes all asset classes and compliance tools from one interface.

slides.title: Asset Creation
slides.subtitle: Guided wizard for tokenising fixed income and equity

slides.title: Bond Tokenisation Wizard
slides.body: The Asset Designer walks issuers through a structured bond tokenisation workflow. Every required parameter, compliance, pricing, supply, is configured before deployment.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/01 Step Bond Tokenisation.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Details and Parameters
slides.body: Issuers configure ISIN, maturity date, coupon structure, and denomination. All parameters feed directly into the smart contract being deployed, no separate developer step required.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/04 Step Instrument Details .jpg

slides.title: Pricing and Valuation Config
slides.body: Each asset is configured with a pricing model and NAV methodology. The platform handles both market-priced instruments and NAV-based assets like funds or deposits with equal precision.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/05 Step Bond pricing and valuation .jpg

slides.title: Modular Compliance Framework
slides.body: Compliance is selected at design time. Operators choose from: investor count caps, supply limits, country blocklists and allowlists, transfer approvals, and time locks. Each module is independently configurable.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/06 Step Complaince Modules.jpg

slides.title: Investor Count Controls
slides.body: DALP enforces hard investor count caps directly on-chain, supporting the 500-investor rule for private bonds, Reg D exemptions, and equivalent local requirements without manual tracking.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/14 Step Investor count.jpg

slides.title: Jurisdiction Controls
slides.body: Country-level blocklists and allowlists restrict investors based on country of residence. Supports sanctions compliance, MiFID II geographic restrictions, and domestic-only issuance, enforced at the smart contract level.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/12 Step Country Blocklist.jpg

slides.title: Review, Approve and Deploy
slides.body: Before deployment, the wizard presents a full review summary for operator sign-off. Once confirmed, the asset deploys on-chain in a single transaction. No separate developer pipeline.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/20 Step Review and Deploy.jpg

slides.title: Equity Tokenisation
slides.body: The same Asset Designer supports equity instruments. Share class configuration, voting rights, and dividend parameters follow the same guided wizard, ensuring consistent compliance across all asset types.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/01 Step.jpg

slides.title: Token Operations
slides.subtitle: Minting, transfers, freeze, and forced transfer controls

slides.title: Token Minting
slides.body: Authorised operators initiate token minting through a step-by-step workflow. Each minting action is subject to the asset's configured approval rules, supporting maker-checker controls in regulated environments.

slides.title: Token Transfers
slides.body: Token transfers include built-in compliance checks. The platform validates investor eligibility, jurisdiction rules, and transfer approval requirements before any transfer settles, preventing non-compliant movements.

slides.title: Freeze and Unfreeze Controls
slides.body: Operators freeze token holdings in response to AML alerts, regulatory holds, or court orders. Frozen tokens remain visible but non-transferable. The unfreeze workflow requires explicit approval, creating a full audit trail.

slides.title: Forced Transfer
slides.body: DALP supports court-ordered and regulatory forced transfers, allowing competent authorities to move tokens between holders. The multi-step workflow ensures full governance controls on this power-action.

slides.title: Settlement and Add-ons
slides.subtitle: Atomic XvP settlement and yield distribution

slides.title: XvP Settlement Overview
slides.body: DALP's XvP add-on enables atomic delivery-versus-payment settlement. Both legs of the trade settle simultaneously on-chain, eliminating counterparty risk and manual reconciliation.

slides.title: XvP Settlement Flow
slides.body: The settlement workflow guides both parties through asset leg configuration, counterparty confirmation, and atomic execution. Each step requires explicit approval, maintaining a complete audit trail.

slides.title: Settlement Finality
slides.body: Once both parties confirm, settlement executes atomically. Both legs succeed or neither does. The completed flow is permanently recorded on-chain, instant finality, no central clearing party needed.

slides.title: Yield and Income Distribution
slides.body: The Yield add-on manages coupon payments, dividend distributions, and periodic income events. The dashboard shows distribution schedules, pending payments, and historical yield events linked to on-chain positions.

slides.title: Governance and Audit
slides.subtitle: Action tracking, approvals, and immutable audit trails

slides.title: Action Queue and Approvals
slides.body: Pending actions requiring approval are queued and visible to authorised users. Maker-checker workflows ensure sensitive operations are reviewed before execution, supporting four-eyes principle requirements.

slides.title: Activity Log and Audit Trail
slides.body: Every platform event is logged: identity verifications, token transfers, compliance changes, settlement events. Regulators and auditors get a complete, tamper-proof record of all platform activity.

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Technical architecture and process flow references

slides.title: DALP Token Lifecycle
slides.body: This diagram traces the complete token lifecycle from asset design and compliance configuration through on-chain deployment, token minting, holder transfers, income distribution events, ongoing compliance checks, and final redemption. Each stage maps to a governed workflow within DALP.

slides.title: Modular Compliance Architecture
slides.body: Every transfer request passes through a configurable compliance policy layer. Six independent modules, investor count caps, supply limits, country blocklists, allowlists, transfer approvals, and time locks, each evaluate the request. The transfer proceeds only when all active modules approve it. A single rejection blocks the transaction.

slides.title: Atomic XvP Settlement Sequence
slides.body: The sequence diagram shows the interaction between Seller, DALP, and Buyer during atomic settlement. Both parties submit their respective legs. DALP validates both simultaneously. If validation passes, asset and payment transfers execute atomically. If either leg fails, the entire settlement is cancelled and both parties are notified.

slides.title: Token Lifecycle Operations
slides.body: Operators initiate token actions including minting, transfers, and freeze operations. Each action passes through a configurable approval gate supporting maker-checker workflows. Approved actions execute on-chain and generate a permanent audit record. Rejected actions are blocked and logged for governance review.

slides.title: DALP Platform Architecture
slides.body: The platform follows a layered architecture. The web application connects through an API layer to the core platform, which orchestrates five key modules: Asset Manager, Compliance Engine, Identity and KYC, Settlement XvP, and Yield Manager. All modules write to and read from the underlying EVM blockchain for finality and transparency.
